channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Concerns persist over Australia’s aged care Integrated Assessment Tool amid partial government reforms
item.description: A federal government decision to partially modify Australia’s Integrated Assessment Tool (IAT) for aged care has intensified debate over the role of automated systems in determining access to support ...

item.title: Suspected quadcopter drone strike in Bajaur kills three members of same family, injures ten
item.description: In Bajaur district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, three people, including a five-year-old girl and a 15-year-old boy, lost their lives while ten others were injured in what police suspect was a drone-based at...

item.title: Fact check: Suan denies viral claim he called VP Sara unwelcome in Cagayan de Oro
item.description: This fact-check clarifies a viral claim circulating on social media alleging that Cagayan de Oro 1st District Representative Lordan Suan declared Vice President Sara Duterte as “unwelcome” in Cagayan ...

item.title: Couple clash over household hygiene habits involving hair, nails and contact lenses
item.description: This article presents a domestic disagreement between a couple, Martin and Debbie, about cleanliness standards in their shared flat. Martin is increasingly uncomfortable with what he describes as Debb...

item.title: Bafana Bafana receive hero’s welcome at OR Tambo after historic 2026 World Cup run
item.description: Bafana Bafana returned home to a warm and emotional reception at OR Tambo International Airport in Johannesburg following their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign. Early on Thursday morning, thousands of su...

item.title: Safety guidance issued for swimming in lakes and rivers amid heat wave in Canada
item.description: As a heat wave brings high temperatures to parts of Canada, particularly Ontario and Quebec where temperatures are expected to reach the mid-30s Celsius, safety advocates and public health experts are...

item.title: Alex Mwakideu criticises shift towards cashless payments in businesses
item.description: Media personality Alex Mwakideu has sparked public debate in Kenya after expressing concern over the growing trend of businesses refusing to accept cash payments. In a post shared on his social media ...

item.title: Nigeria’s economic reforms boost markets but increase political pressure on President Tinubu
item.description: Nigeria’s recent economic reforms under President Bola Tinubu have delivered strong performance in financial markets, but dem dey also create serious political tension as many citizens struggle with r...

item.title: Supreme Court flags risks of unverified AI use in judiciary and sets aside NCLT-NCLAT orders over fake citations
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has raised serious concerns over the unchecked use of artificial intelligence in judicial proceedings, particularly warning that unverified or AI-generated content can sever...

item.title: Kerala government to allow CBI prosecution of former KSCDC officials in cashew import irregularities case
item.description: The Kerala government has informed the Kerala High Court that it has decided to grant sanction to the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to prosecute two former top officials of the Kerala State Ca...

item.title: EJ Obiena clinches back-to-back gold medals in Europe after return from competition break
item.description: Filipino pole vault star EJ Obiena has made a strong return to international competition after nearly three months away, securing back-to-back gold medals in Europe. Competing in the Austrian Open hel...

item.title: Harry Styles joins Wembley crowd in celebrating England’s World Cup victory during concert screening
item.description: Harry Styles marked England’s dramatic World Cup victory over DR Congo while performing at Wembley Stadium as part of his ongoing residency tour. The pop star is currently headlining a record-breaking...

item.title: Tanzania 2026/27 Works Budget and Economic Performance Update: Mining, Dividends, Investment and Infrastructure Expansion
item.description: Tanzania’s latest economic and infrastructure update highlights strong performance across key sectors including mining, public enterprises, investment inflows, and capital markets, alongside a major i...

item.title: NAB transfers recovered assets worth Rs4.1 billion to Rawalpindi Development Authority in embezzlement case
item.description: The National Accountability Bureau (NAB) Islamabad has formally handed over recovered assets worth over Rs4.1 billion to the Rawalpindi Development Authority (RDA) in a major embezzlement case involvi...

item.title: Pauline Hanson rules out Moira Deeming joining One Nation amid political dispute
item.description: The live political coverage reports that One Nation leader Pauline Hanson has ruled out Victorian Liberal MP Moira Deeming from joining her party. Hanson stated that Deeming would not be welcome in On...

item.title: African Investors Urged to Focus on Long-Term Risks Despite Middle East Ceasefire
item.description: This opinion article argues that although the recent ceasefire framework in the Middle East has temporarily calmed global markets, African investors should not assume that economic risks have disappea...

item.title: Bafana Bafana return to South Africa after historic 2026 FIFA World Cup media briefing
item.description: The South African national football team, Bafana Bafana, held a recorded media briefing following their return from the United States after participating in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The briefing, shar...

item.title: Six national team coaches exit after disappointing 2026 FIFA World Cup campaigns
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has once again shown say the tournament no dey spare coaches, as six national team managers end up losing their jobs after disappointing campaigns. The article explains how the...

item.title: Ten-year-old pupil found dead after leaving special needs school in Ishikawa, Japan
item.description: A ten-year-old boy who went missing from a special needs school in Komatsu city, Ishikawa Prefecture, Japan, has been found dead after a large-scale search operation. The child left his classroom at t...

item.title: Labrador, Pangasinan designates July 12 as West Philippine Sea Victory Day to mark 2016 arbitral ruling
item.description: A coastal municipality in Pangasinan, specifically Labrador, has officially declared July 12 of every year as “West Philippine Sea Victory Day” through an executive order issued by Mayor Noel Uson. Th...
